name: North Thai Tomato-&-Meat Sauce (Nam Prik Ong)
recipeIngredient: 2 Serrano chilies, with seeds and chopped
1 t Dried shrimp, minced - or 1/2 tsp. shrimp paste
or 1/2 cup Defatted chicken stock or water
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the pork of fat and membrane, then cut into chunks.
2. Soak dried shrimp in warm water for 30 minutes, drain, and mince.
3. Place pork in a food processor and pulse until ground. Alternatively, chop with a sharp knife.
4. Heat oil in a heavy, medium-sized saucepan over medium-high heat.
5. Add garlic and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes or until browned.
6. Add shallots, chilies, dried shrimp or shrimp paste, and lemon grass, then stir-fry for 30 seconds.
7. Add the pork and stir-fry for about 1 minute or until browned.
8. Add tomatoes, stock or water, fish sauce, and sugar. Increase heat to high.
9. Cook, stirring frequently, for 2 minutes or until the mixture boils vigorously.
10. Reduce heat to low and simmer, uncovered, for 15-20 minutes or until slightly thickened.
11. Serve with sticky rice.
Serves 4 as a main dish or 6 in combination with other dishes.
64 CALORIES FOR EACH OF 6 SERVINGS: 6 G PROTEIN, 3 G FAT, 5 G CARBOHYDRATE; 127 MG SODIUM; 15 MG CHOLESTEROL.
